title: On Learning Gaussian Multi-index Models with Gradient Flow Part I: General Properties and Two-timescale Learning
venue: Communications on Pure and Applied Mathematics, 2025
abstract: We study gradient flow on the multi-index regression problem for high-dimensional Gaussian data. Multi-index functions consist of a composition of an unknown low-rank linear projection and an arbitrary unknown, low-dimensional link function. We consider a two-timescale algorithm and establish global convergence of the resulting Grassmannian population gradient flow dynamics, providing a quantitative description of its saddle-to-saddle dynamics.

title: Variational Inference for Uncertainty Quantification: an Analysis of Trade-offs
abstract: We show that under the mean-field assumption, any factorized approximation can correctly estimate at most one of the marginal variances, the marginal precisions, or the generalized variance.

title: Convergence of Shallow ReLU Networks on Weakly Interacting Data
Joint Learning in the Gaussian Single Index Model
abstract: We consider the problem of jointly learning a one-dimensional projection and a univariate function in high-dimensional Gaussian models.

title: Computational-Statistical Gaps in Gaussian Single-Index Models
abstract: We show that computationally efficient algorithms, within the Statistical Query and Low-Degree Polynomial frameworks, necessarily require a number of samples determined by a generative exponent associated with the model.

title: Batch and Match: Black-box Variational Inference with a Score-based Divergence
abstract: We propose batch and match (BaM), an alternative approach to black-box variational inference based on a score-based divergence. This divergence can be optimized by a closed-form proximal update for Gaussian variational families with full covariance matrices.

title: Stochastic Differential Equations models for Least-Squares SGD
On Single Index Models beyond Gaussian Data
On the Spectral Bias of Two-layer Linear Networks
SGD with Large Step Sizes Learns Sparse Features
abstract: We present empirical observations that large step sizes may lead iterates to jump from one side of a valley to the other causing loss stabilization, and this induces a hidden stochastic dynamics that biases implicitly toward simple predictors. These observations unveil how gradient and noise drive together the SGD dynamics.

title: Kernelized Diffusion Maps
abstract: We build a different estimator of the Laplacian, via a reproducing kernel Hilbert space method, which adapts naturally to the regularity of the problem. We provide non-asymptotic statistical rates proving that the kernel estimator can circumvent the curse of dimensionality.

title: Label Noise (Stochastic) Gradient Descent Implicitly Solves the Lasso for Quadratic Parametrisation
abstract: We study the role of the label noise in the training dynamics of a quadratically parametrised model. We explicitly characterise the solution chosen by the stochastic flow and prove that it implicitly solves a Lasso program.

title: Gradient Flow Dynamics of Shallow ReLU Networks for Square Loss and Orthogonal Inputs
abstract: We present a precise description of the gradient flow dynamics of training one-hidden layer ReLU neural networks for the mean squared error at small initialisation. We show convergence to zero loss and characterise the implicit bias towards minimum variation norm, with a saddle to saddle dynamics.

title: Last Iterate Convergence of SGD for Least-Squares in the Interpolation Regime
abstract: We study the noiseless model in the least-squares setup and give explicit non-asymptotic convergence rates in the over-parameterized setting.

title: Implicit Bias of SGD for Diagonal Linear Networks: a Provable Benefit of Stochasticity
abstract: We highlight that structured noise can induce better generalisation and help explain the greater performances observed in practice of stochastic gradient descent over gradient descent.

title: Overcoming the Curse of Dimensionality with Laplacian Regularization in Semi-supervised Learning
abstract: We provide a statistical analysis to overcome the curse of dimensionality in Laplacian regularization for semi-supervised learning, and unveil spectral filtering methods with desirable behaviors.

title: Statistical Estimation of the Poincaré Constant and Application to Sampling Multimodal Distributions
abstract: We show that given sufficiently many samples of a measure, one can estimate its Poincaré constant, and derive an algorithm that captures a low dimensional representation of the data.

title: Statistical Optimality of Stochastic Gradient Descent on Hard Learning Problems through Multiple Passes
abstract: We show that for hard problems, multiple passes of SGD lead to statistically optimal predictions while single pass does not, and the optimal number of passes increases with sample size.

title: Exponential Convergence of Testing Error for Stochastic Gradient Methods
abstract: We show that while the excess testing loss converges slowly to zero, the testing error converges exponentially fast if low-noise conditions are assumed.

title: Central Limit Theorem for Stationary Fleming-Viot Particle Systems in Finite Spaces
venue: ALEA, Latin American Journal of Probability and Mathematical Statistics, 2018
abstract: We establish a central limit theorem for the empirical distribution of stationary Fleming-Viot particle systems in finite state spaces.
